# DGE Template: Go/No-Go Decision

## Overview
Evaluate Go/No-Go for investments, new ventures, or major pivots.

## Recommended Characters
Columbo + Gekko (finance) + Charlie Brown (scope) + Steve Jobs (boldness)

## Scene Structure

### Scene 1: Market & Customer
```
Columbo:    "Have you talked to customers? How many?"
Gekko:      "Show me TAM/SAM/SOM. Numbers, not feelings."
Charlie Brown: "...maybe interview 10 companies first..."
Steve Jobs: "The market won't wait. Move now."

Gaps: customer interview count, market size data, target clarity
```

### Scene 2: Financials & Risk
```
Columbo:    "How much do we lose if this fails?"
Gekko:      "Show me IRR, NPV, payback period."
Charlie Brown: "...can we start with $2M...?"
Steve Jobs: "If you can't take risks, you gain nothing."

Gaps: investment amount, return projections, worst case, exit criteria
```

### Scene 3: Team & Resources
```
Columbo:    "Who's doing this? Is the current team enough?"
Gekko:      "Have you calculated the impact on existing business?"
Charlie Brown: "...keep it to what 3 people can handle..."

Gaps: headcount needed, impact on existing business, hiring plan
```

### Scene 4: Competition & Differentiation
```
Columbo:    "Is anyone else doing the same thing?"
Gekko:      "What if a competitor comes with 10x the funding?"
Steve Jobs: "What's our weapon that nobody else has?"

Gaps: competitive analysis, differentiation, barriers to entry
```
